_README: Optional overrides for Hub Diagnostics device classification. Most integrations need NO entry here: the connection type is derived from the hub's own isNetwork flag (LAN => lan_direct, otherwise cloud) and the integration name from the parent-app name. Add an entry ONLY when that derivation is wrong. Keys are substrings matched CASE-INSENSITIVELY (the app lowercases them for you) against a device's parent-app name, or its DRIVER TYPE NAME for a standalone device with no parent app. 'conn' is one of: homekit, lan_direct, lan_bridge, cloud, virtual, hubmesh, other (the radios zigbee/zwave/matter/bluetooth are auto-detected). TWO MODES: (1) 'conn' ALONE is a connection-type-only fix -- the device stays STANDALONE: counted in the Connection Types breakdown but OMITTED from the Integrations breakdown. Use this for single cloud/LAN drivers (Awair, Pushover, a TV) that aren't really an integration. (2) 'conn' + 'name' labels the device as part of the named integration and groups it (e.g. all Blink* drivers as 'Blink'). To use: upload this file to the hub's File Manager as hub_diagnostics_integration_overrides.json, then open Hub Diagnostics and Save the settings page (or click Clear Cache) to apply. Any key starting with an underscore is ignored, so every entry below is inactive: remove the leading underscore to enable one, edit to taste, and delete the rest.
